free_text: Excited states in 14N from 12C+d reactions.
Facility is not given.
Solid state detectors having a resolution of
around 50 to 60 keV were used to detect the reaction
products and a fixed counter at 90 degr. served as a
monitor.
The targets used were self supporting carbon targets
having a thickness corresponding to 5-10 mu-g/cm2.
Carbon build up on the targets was kept below 5% during
the whole period for the measurement of one excitation
curve, by a liquid air trap before the target and also
by allowing the beam to fall on the target only during
measurement.
